{"id":"project-LICHEN-ak04","title":"Silent protobuf encoding failure in create_routing_error","description":"File: rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/bridge.rs:385

The create_routing_error function silently ignores protobuf encoding failures:

```rust
routing.encode(&mut payload).ok();
```

The .ok() call converts the Result to Option and discards any error. If encoding fails, the function still returns a MeshPacket with an empty or corrupted payload, which could cause:

1. Peer nodes to receive malformed routing error packets
2. Silent routing failures with no diagnostic information
3. Protocol violations since the packet claims to have routing data but the payload is empty

FIX: Either propagate the error by making the function return Result, or use expect/unwrap since encoding to a growable Vec should never fail in practice (adding a comment explaining why).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:34:33.114315Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:34:33.114353Z"}
